LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALLIED MEAT SERVICE, INC, ANTHONY NIGRO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AWARDED $6,465 IN ATTORNEY FEES FOR THOSE ACTIVITIES AND EXPENSES REASONABLY RELATED TO DEFAULT. REQUEST FOR DELAY IN LIFTING THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT DENIED. THE COURT ADDS: THE $6,465 IS AGAINST MR. SABAHAT. THE $6,465 IS DUE WITHIN NINETY DAYS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ALLIED MEAT SERVICE, INC, ANTHONY NIGRO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT. Robert H. Staley, Esq. of Epstein, Englert, Staley & Coffey (415) 398-6392 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; William S. Weisberg, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller (415) 296-7070 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Evidentiary objections overruled. Plaintiff's counsel awarded $6,465 in attorney fees for those activities and expenses reasonably related to default. Request for delay in lifting the default and default judgment denied. The Court adds: the $6,465 is against Mr. Sabahat. The $6,465 is due within ninety days. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Brenda Fields, #7235.

Lillian Wolf, Esq. appeared for plaintiff B&B Quality Food Providers.
Ron Guidi, plaintiff's Accounts Receivable Manager, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Allied Meat Service, Inc. or Anthony R. Nigro.
Defaults were entered against defendants on March 27, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, September 3, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff B&B Quality Food Providers, and against the Defaulted Defendants Allied Meat Serice, Inc. and Anthony R. Nigro as follows:

Damages/Principal - $60,542.40.
Prejudgment Interest - $14,230.63.
Attorney's Fees - $2,367.00.

Total - $77,140.03.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
